(Department of Agriculture , Co-operation and Farmers Welfare) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 15th February, 2016 G.S.R.163(E).—The following draft rules further to amend the Insecticides Rules, 1971, which the Central Government proposes to make in exercise of the powers conferred by section 36 of the Insecticides Act, 1968 (46 of 1968), is hereby published, as required by the said section, for information of all persons likely to be affected thereby; and notice is hereby given that the said draft rules shall be taken into consideration after the expiry of forty-five days from the date on which copies of this notification as published in the Gazette of India are made available to the public; The objections or suggestions which may be received from any person in respect of the draft rules before the expiry of the aforesaid period of forty-five days shall be considered by the Central Government. Objections and suggestions in respect to the draft rules may be forwarded to the Joint Secretary (Plant Protection), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Department of Agriculture, Co-operation and Farmers Welfare, Krishi Bhawan, New Delhi-110 001 or send it to the e-mail address of the undersigned at : js- ppagri@nic.in. DRAFT RULES 1. (1) These rules may be called the Insecticides (Second Amendment) Rules, 2016. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their final publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Insecticides Rules, 1971 (hereinafter referred as the said rules) in rule 18, in sub rule (l), (a) in clause (j) for the words “printed on the leaflet”, the words “ printed on one place on the leaflet” shall be substituted. (b) in clause (k), the word “detailed” shall be omitted. (c) in clause (m), (i) for sub-clause (i), the following sub-clause shall be substituted, namely:- “whether conditions in general” (ii) for sub-clause (ii), the following sub-clause shall be substituted, namely:- “soil ,wherever applicable” (iii) for sub-clause (vii), the following sub-clause shall be substituted, namely:- “ dosage per hectare (include gram active ingredient formulation and dilution in litres )” (d) in clause (u), the words “secured to primary pack or” shall be inserted after the word “shall be”; 3. In the said rules , in rule 19,for sub-rule (1), the following sub-rule shall be substituted, namely:- “(1) (i) The label shall have either one ,two or three panels and shall be printed only in English and Hindi languages unless stated otherwise. The minimum size of the one and the two panels label to be affixed on the face of the container shall be not less than 110 square centimetres. The minimum size of the three panel label shall not be less than 160 square centimetres. There shall be three types of labels depending on the net content/volume: (i) Ultra small packs-1g/ml to 50 g/ml (ii) Small packs-51 g/ml to 250 g/ml (iii) Big packs->250 g/ml The following information shall be printed on the label :- (a) name of insecticide (brand name or trade name under which the insecticide is sold). The brand name should not be of bigger size than the common name and it should occupy not more than 20% of area of the face or panel. The brand name could be in BOLD (b) common name; -- 9 of 15 -- 10 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (c) instructions to “Read Leaflet Before Use”; in bold and in all languages where it is proposed for sale and to be placed on the top of the label in a prominent place (d) list of crops and broad category of pests on which it is registered to apply; (e) purpose of the chemical should be mentioned on the label, For example:- (i) For Agricultural Use (ii) For Pest Control Operators (iii) For House Hold Use (iv) For Public Health” (f) optimal re-entry period after each application of pesticide ; (g) safety precautions (h) composition of the formulation (i) Symptoms of poisoning and first aid (j) antidote statement (k) name of the manufacturer (l) imported and re-packed by (m) marketed by (n) registration number of the insecticide (o) manufacturing license number (p) batch number (q) manufacturing date (r) expiry date (s) Maximum Retail Price (M.R.P.) Inclusive of taxes (t) net content of volume (in metric system) (u) cautionary symbol as per the toxicity classification and cautionary words (v) Warning (i) Not to be used on crops and pests other than those mentioned on label and leaflets (ii) Not to be used for Post-Harvest Applications. (iii) Destroy the container after use as directed on leaflet. (iv) 4. Directions and dosage for poly houses and confined area - Refer to the leaflet. (v) Any other warning or cautionary statement as approved by Registration Committee ( w). Company’s name, barcode, hologram, QR Code, e-mail id and customer care numbers. Additional information can be added to the Label after approval by the Registration Committee. All relevant safety pictograms (refer picture I in the THIRD SCHEDULE) of size not less than one square centimetre at the bottom of the label in respect of labels of minimum size. Pictograms shall be proportionately bigger in respect of labels of bigger size at the bottom of the right side panel. They shall be printed only once on the label. For ultra small and small labels the pictograms on the primary pack shall be printed only on the secondary pack 1(ii) The label shall be printed legibly using font size of not less than seven points (7) in English and seven points (7) in Hindi and regional languages. -- 10 of 15 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 11 1(iii)(a) If the container or packet or sachet is small, the product should be packed in a secondary pack on which label of prescribed size as per contents of clause (i) of sub-rule (1) of rule 19 shall be printed. (iii)(b) Bigger packs of existing registrations may also opt for packing the product in a secondary pack on which label of prescribed size as per contents of clause (i) of sub-rule (1) of rule 19 shall be printed.” 1(iv)(a) In case where small sachets, packets or containers (for sizes between 51 and 250 g/ml) are used shall be further packed in a secondary pack. The following details shall be printed with indelible ink on primary pack in English and Hindi and in the font size of not less than five points (5) as well as on the secondary pack in English and Hindi and in font size of not less than seven points (7) :- (a) brand name (b) common name (c) manufacturers name and address (d) imported and re-packed by (if applicable) (e) antidote (f) registration number (g) manufacturer’s license number (h) batch number (i) manufacturing date (j) expiry date (k) maximum retail price (MRP incl. of taxes, in rupees) (l) net content (m) customer care details and (n) cautionary symbol as per the toxicity classification and cautionary words. (iv)(b) In case where ultra small sachets, packets or containers (for sizes between 1 and 50 g/ml) are used shall be further packed in a secondary pack. The following details shall be printed with indelible ink on the inner most pack in English only and in the font size of not less than five points (5) as well as on the secondary pack in English and Hindi and in font size of not less than seven points (7) :- (a) brand name; (b) common name; (c) manufacturers name (d) imported and re-packed by (if applicable); (e) antidote (f) registration number; (g) manufacturer’s license number; (h) batch number; (i) manufacturing date; (j) expiry date; (k) net content ; The secondary pack will have additional information printed like (l) maximum retail price (MRP incl. of taxes, in rupees) (m) customer care details; and (n) cautionary symbol as per the toxicity classification and cautionary words 1(v) The model labels (two panel and three panel) shall be as per the pictures II, III, IV & V given in the “ THIRD SCHEDULE ’’ .
